creator: Alexander Terp
description: fn return like 'return { "due": due, "message": "hi" }' types as { str: int|str } instead of struct map { "due": int, "message": str }, so parsed.due picks up a spurious str arm and draws hints like 'int|dynamic|str - int' (remind L509, last remaining #139 type hint). Builtins already produce struct-map types (e.g. parse_epoch), so the machinery exists; the gap is map literals / user fn returns. Needs design thought on when to keep literal keys vs widen (e.g. dynamic-keyed writes).
title: rad check: map literals widen to value-join, losing per-key types
